name: fractal-skill-evals
description: Behavioral evals for the fractal decomposition skill — tests skill-specific mechanics (leaf detection, budget enforcement, scope isolation, parallel solve, reunification)

evals.name: End-to-end design decomposition
evals.description: Fractal decompose on a system design task produces a tree of sub-specs and a unified design document
assertions: A .fractal/{work-id}/ directory is created with manifest.json
root.md contains the original task
tree/ contains at least 3 child directories with spec.md files
At least one child is further decomposed (depth >= 2)
All leaf nodes have result.md files after solve phase
Non-leaf nodes have result.md after reunification
output.md exists with the final merged result
manifest.json shows status: complete with tree metrics

evals.name: Simple tasks detected as leaves without splitting
evals.description: A task that is already atomic should be detected as a LEAF and solved directly without unnecessary decomposition
assertions: DECOMPOSE VERDICT is LEAF with a reason
No child directories created under tree/
The task is solved directly
manifest.json shows tree_depth: 1 and leaf_nodes: 1

evals.name: Node budget caps total tree size
evals.description: When max_total_nodes would be exceeded, remaining nodes are forced to become leaves
assertions: Total nodes in the tree does not exceed max_total_nodes
At least one node that could have been split was forced to LEAF
manifest.json records the budget limit was hit
All forced leaves still get solved

evals.name: Leaf nodes solved in parallel
evals.description: When solve_mode is parallel, all independent leaf nodes are spawned as concurrent subagents
assertions: Multiple Agent tool calls appear in the same message for leaf solving
Each solve subagent receives correct scope and boundaries
Sibling specs are included as read-only context
All SOLVE REPORTs are collected and recorded

evals.name: Sibling scopes do not overlap
evals.description: Each child sub-spec has explicit boundaries and does not encroach on sibling scopes
assertions: Each child spec.md has a 'boundaries' section listing what it does NOT handle
No two siblings claim the same responsibility
Solve results stay within declared scope
Reunification step identifies any scope leakage

evals.name: Code task decomposed and implemented
evals.description: A coding task is decomposed into sub-specs, each solved in an isolated worktree, then code merged via reunification
assertions: SOLVE subagents run with isolation: worktree
Each leaf commits with fractal({slug}): prefix
REUNIFY integrates code from all leaves
Final code compiles/passes tests
output.md references all files changed

evals.name: Reunification detects and resolves interface conflicts
evals.description: When sibling solutions have mismatched interfaces, the reunify step detects and resolves them
assertions: REUNIFY REPORT shows CONFLICTS field with at least one conflict
RESOLUTION field explains how each conflict was resolved
Merged result is internally consistent
STATUS is MERGED (not CONFLICT) after resolution
